zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Harris於1625年1月1日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 愛德華加斯托時，她的父親Capt Thomas Harris Sr39歲，母親Adria Hoare11622歲。她於大約1649年與Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1703年3月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可享年78歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Harris于1625年1月1日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 愛德華加斯托时，她的父亲Capt Thomas Harris Sr39岁，母亲Adria Hoare11622岁。她于大约1649年与Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1703年3月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可享年78岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可。

nameMeanings.name: Harris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Harry
meanings: English (mainly south Wales and southwestern England): from the medieval personal name Harry, a common form of Henry , with assimilation of -nr- to -rr- and regular Middle English change of -er- to -ar-. In south Wales the surname is generally a post-medieval coinage, in most cases not hereditary until the 18th or 19th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hariric, composed of the elements hari, heri ‘army’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Harry (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Haris
meanings: English and Welsh (Glamorgan): variant of   Harris  .
Muslim: from the Arabic personal name based on ḥārith, ḥāris ‘plowman, cultivator’. Compare Hares .

variants.name: Harries
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Elfriede, Wolfgang.
North German and East Frisian: variant of   Harjes  .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: English, French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Jamaica and Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements haim, heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’, introduced to England by the Normans in the form Henri. During the Middle Ages this name became enormously popular in England and was borne by eight kings. Continental forms of the personal name were equally popular throughout Europe. In the period in which the majority of English surnames were formed, a common English vernacular form of the name was Harry, hence the surnames Harris (southern) and Harrison (northern). Official documents of the period normally used the Latinized form Henricus. In medieval times, English Henry absorbed an originally distinct Old English personal name that had hagan ‘hawthorn’ (compare Hain 2) as its first element, and there has also been confusion with Amery. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. German Heinrich , and also their derivatives, e.g. Swedish Henriksson (see Henrikson ). Compare Henri .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hInnéirghe ‘descendant of Innéirghe’, a byname based on éirghe ‘arising’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Éinrí or Mac Einri, patronymics from the personal names Éinrí, Einri, Irish forms of Henry. It is also found as a variant of McEnery .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Harris was born on 1 January 1625, in Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Capt Thomas Harris Sr, was 39 and her mother, Adria Hoare, was 11622. She married Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon about 1649, in Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 18 March 1703, in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 78, and was buried in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Harris est née le 1 janvier 1625, à Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Capt Thomas Harris Sr, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Adria Hoare, avait 11622 ans. Elle a épousé Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon environ 1649, à Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 mars 1703, à Henrico, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Henrico, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Harris am 1. Januar 1625 in Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginia, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Capt Thomas Harris Sr 39 und ihre Mutter Adria Hoare 11622. Sie hat um 1649 in Virginia, British Colonial America Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 18. März 1703 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Harris è nata il il 1 gennaio 1625 nel Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginia, British Colonial America, sua padre, Capt Thomas Harris Sr, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Adria Hoare, aveva 11622 anni. Lei sposato Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon circa 1649, nel Virginia, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 18 marzo 1703, nel Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Harrisが1625年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州プリンス エドワードジャムストウンで生まれた時，父Capt Thomas Harris Srは39歳，母Adria Hoareは11622歳であった。約1649年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州でLt. Col. Thomas Lygonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1703年3月18日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ヘンリコで78歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ヘンリコにて埋葬された。

ko.Henrico County, Virginia, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 찰스 시티 카운티 맬버 힐
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Harris(이)가 1625년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 플린스 에드워드 자메스타운에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Capt Thomas Harris Sr(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adria Hoare(은)는 11622세였다. 그녀는 대략 1649년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에서 Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1703년 3월 18일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Harris nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1625, em Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Capt Thomas Harris Sr, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Adria Hoare, tinha 11622 anos. Ela casou-se com Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon aproximadamente 1649, em Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 18 de março de 1703, em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Harris родилась 1 января 1625 г. в местности джеместоун, принц эдвард, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Capt Thomas Harris Sr было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Adria Hoare, 11622. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon около 1649 г. в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 18 марта 1703 г. в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Harris nació el 1 de enero de 1625, en Jamestown, Prince Edward, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Capt Thomas Harris Sr, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Adria Hoare, tenía 11622 años. Se casó con Lt. Col. Thomas Lygon aproximadamente 1649, en Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 18 de marzo de 1703, en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.
